behaviors.description: Review input is scoped to changed lines (diff hunks), not full modified file contents.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (diff parser -> review-input builder), not just isolated logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; fixture diffs are enough. MOCK-HIDE: pure unit test of parser can miss leakage when assembler re-attaches full file text. LLM-DEP: none; this checks input construction, not model judgment. Adversarial critique: this could be Unit if one function alone computes final scoped payload; if code is actually split across parser and prompt-builder, Integration is the safer minimum. Rejection-gate: System/Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text only requires scoping correctness, not real API/model/pipeline execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run diff parsing + review-payload assembly together on multi-file fixtures where full file text is available; assert produced review input contains only changed-line spans/hunks and excludes unchanged sections.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to correct PR diff positions.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: GitHub diff-position semantics are enforced by the real PR API/UI boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked clients often accept invalid/misaligned positions that real API rejects or renders incorrectly. LLM-DEP: none. Adversarial critique: if implementation uses a fully deterministic local mapping library known to mirror GitHub exactly, Integration might suffice; in practice this behavior is explicitly about real diff view placement, so System is minimum. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text requires correctness in the PR diff view, which is external-system behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions, then retrieve review comments from GitHub and verify each comment appears on intended file/hunk/line in the diff context.

behaviors.description: Agent finds at least one real defect in a PR containing a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model judgment (detecting a true defect). Deterministic stubs always pass and cannot validate detection quality. REAL-INFRA: real PR hosting is optional for this behavior; a fixed diff artifact can drive the model call. MULTI-STEP: single model decision stage, so not Workflow by default. Adversarial critique: could be Workflow if detection requires chained model passes (planner->critic); behavior text only asks for at least one detected defect, not multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because no explicit multi-agent or multi-model-step requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Send a known-bug PR diff (golden fixture) through the real model-based analyzer and assert at least one returned finding matches the seeded defect class/location with acceptable tolerance.

behaviors.description: Each summary finding maps to an inline comment ID posted in the same run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs in cross-module state handoff (inline-post results -> summary composer). REAL-INFRA: no need for real network if API responses are stubbed with run-scoped IDs. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing only summary formatter could miss missing/foreign IDs introduced by orchestration layer. LLM-DEP: none. Adversarial critique: could be Unit if one function both stores and serializes mappings; if posting and summarization are separate modules, Integration is minimum. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is about internal run-consistency, not external API semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute orchestration with stubbed inline-post API returning deterministic IDs; then build summary and assert every summary entry references an ID that exists in this run’s posted-inline collection.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped before final summary payload is posted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a pipeline handoff/invariant across collection, dedupe/filter, and payload builder. REAL-INFRA: real HTTP not required to catch loss before posting. MOCK-HIDE: unit tests on final serializer can miss drops in intermediate orchestration stages. LLM-DEP: none. Adversarial critique: could be Unit if drop risk is confined to a single pure transform function; behavior wording ('before posting') implies multi-stage flow, supporting Integration. Rejection-gate: System rejected because loss detection can be proven with in-process wiring and stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end local orchestration from inline findings list through summary payload construction with edge-case fixtures (duplicates, ordering, mixed severities); assert payload cardinality and IDs preserve all inline findings after defined filtering rules.

behaviors.description: Sequence succeeds on a real PR with fixed precomputed findings (read diff -> post inline -> post summary).
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly targets a real PR and live API sequence. MOCK-HIDE: mocks can miss permission/scope issues, API ordering constraints, secondary rate-limit behavior, and real payload validation. LLM-DEP: none because findings are fixed. Adversarial critique: could be Integration if all external effects are abstracted and contract-tested elsewhere; behavior text explicitly requires real pull request success, so System remains minimum. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because model output is not part of this behavior (precomputed findings).
behaviors.test_description: Run automation against a disposable real PR using a fixed findings fixture; verify all three stages complete, inline comments are created, and top-level summary posts successfully.

behaviors.description: Full live run with model-generated findings on a real PR completes correctly without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is a full agent pipeline combining real infrastructure and model-dependent analysis in sequence (read diff -> model analysis -> inline posting -> summary posting). LLM-DEP: yes. REAL-INFRA: yes (real PR/API). MULTI-STEP: yes, agentic decision output is handed to downstream posting/summarization stages end-to-end. Adversarial critique: could be Agentic if treated as a single model-quality check plus trivial deterministic plumbing; behavior text requires complete live run correctness on a real PR, so pipeline-level failures must be catchable. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because it would not necessarily validate full multi-stage execution correctness across the real PR workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute unattended end-to-end run on a real test PR with the production model enabled; assert run completion, non-empty valid findings, successful inline posts, and summary consistency with posted comments.
